sourceMovie.title: Zootopia
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Zootopia for the same reason it broke through in 2016: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Byron Howard keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Puss in Boots
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Zootopia clicked for you, Puss in Boots (2011) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.title: Open Season
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Zootopia clicked for you, Open Season (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Zootopia?
faq.answer: Try Puss in Boots, Open Season, Zootopia 2—each shares DNA with Zootopia while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Zootopia?
faq.answer: Zootopia (2016) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Family, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Zootopia part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Zootopia Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
